creator: tatsuki_makino@hotmail.com
text:
(In reply to Stefan Eßer from comment #7)

Even MASTER_SITES that require ca_root_nss when downloading distfiles will not cause problems in most cases.
Some of the reasons for it are listed below.

+ Most users will have installed ca_root_nss. (Web browser or etc. need it.)
+ If download of the distfiles from https fail, the distfiles will finally be downloaded from MASTER_SITE_BACKUP.

And my
> And it seems to me that the problem is less likely to occur these days, has something changed on the base side?

These days, it seems that several root certificates are installed in the base just like any other OS.
They are located in /usr/share/certs.
But I don't know if they are used when fetching distfile.

To begin with, I don't know why ca_root_nss would be needed for that server :)
Is intermediate certificate relevant or not...?
